design_philosophy.core_loop: Melee-focused combat with gunplay support in fully explorable level spaces. Player chains together martial arts combos and throws against multiple enemy classes while managing limited ammunition and health pickups.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Melee mastery vs. ammunition scarcity. Game incentivizes hand-to-hand combat as primary damage method while offering gun alternatives for tactical variety.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Superhuman martial artist operative uncovering conspiracies in a cyberpunk totalitarian world. Inspired by Ghost in the Shell and Akira—embodying the anime archetype of the augmented agent questioning her creators.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Bungie West made a deliberate design choice to privilege melee combat over ranged gunplay in a third-person action game. This is reinforced by ammunition scarcity and the unlock of progressively stronger combos. The game rejects small-arena combat in favor of fully open, explorable level spaces with architectural complexity (hired actual architects). The narrative frames the player character's superhuman abilities as organic (Daodan Chrysalis) rather than mechanical augmentation, grounding the fantasy in body-horror biology rather than technology.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Enemy tiers provide scaling challenge through color-coded health indicators (green=high health, red=critical). Multiple enemy classes each with distinct unarmed combat styles encourage learning attack patterns. No explicit difficulty selection documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Unlockable melee combos and stronger martial arts moves unlock in later levels as player progresses through fourteen levels. Power-ups (hyposprays for healing, cloaking devices) scattered throughout and dropped by corpses provide temporary stat boosts.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression through fourteen levels unlocks new move sets and access to previously restricted areas.
replayability_hooks: Fully explorable open level spaces encourage discovery of alternative routes
Multiple enemy classes with distinct combat styles reward mastery of matchup knowledge
Scarce ammunition encourages varied playstyle experimentation on replay

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through fully explorable architectural spaces (buildings designed by professional architects)
Dialogue delivered by player character Konoko (voice acted by Amanda Winn-Lee) during gameplay progression
Cinematic framing and anime-inspired visual style (Ghost in the Shell, Akira influence)
Lore exposition through gameplay: player discovers secrets about Konoko's past and true nature incrementally

faction_indicators: Technological Crimes Task Force (TCTF) - totalitarian government police force (player's initial employer)
The Syndicate - monolithic criminal organization rival to TCTF
World Coalition Government - single totalitarian entity controlling all nations
danger_signals: Player character discovers employers concealing secrets about her past and origins
Government's totalitarian control: misrepresenting toxic regions as 'wilderness preserves' to populace
Atmospheric Conversion Centers—critical infrastructure whose sabotage threatens global population survival
Syndicate plot to catastrophically malfunction air-treatment plants
environmental_storytelling: Heavily polluted Earth in year 2032 with minimal habitable regions (visual worldbuilding)
Urban cyberpunk architecture across fourteen fully explorable level spaces
Color-coded enemy tiers and health indicators provide visual feedback on combat threat
Corpses dropping weapons and power-ups reinforce scarcity/resource management narrative
